channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: White House says it has the right to punish AP reporters over Gulf naming dispute
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The White House said Wednesday that news organizations that refuse to use President Donald Trump’s new name for the Gulf of Mexico were telling “lies” and insisted it would continue to bar Associated Press journalists from presidential events.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former longtime Illinois legislative leader Michael Madigan is convicted in corruption trial
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — A Chicago Democrat who once set much of Illinois’ political agenda as the longest-serving legislative leader in U.S. history was convicted of conspiracy, bribery and wire fraud Wednesday in a split verdict in his high-profile corruption trial.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Starbucks accused of violating anti-discrimination laws: Missouri AG
item.description: The lawsuit alleges that initiatives designed to prioritize diversity, equity, and inclusion ultimately amount to unlawful discrimination.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, NewsNation

item.title: MD, Baltimore mayor target Glock for selling pistols that can be converted into machine guns
item.description: The Maryland Attorney General and mayor of Baltimore City filed a lawsuit against Glock, Inc. for continuing to sell handguns that can be converted into machine guns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump upends US policy on Ukraine and says he and Putin have agreed to begin talks on ending the war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump upended three years of U.S. policy toward Ukraine on Wednesday, saying that he and Russian leader Vladimir Putin had agreed to begin negotiations on ending the war following a sudden prisoner swap.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Valentine’s Day scams to watch for this week
item.description: As Valentine's Day approaches, scammers are looking to deceive people. Here are the top scams to be aware of, and how to avoid falling for them.
item.dc:creator: Zach Myers, NewsNation

item.title: Canada’s new fentanyl czar says goal is to totally stop the drug from entering the US
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — The man appointed by the Canadian government to help stop fentanyl from entering the United States said his goal is to totally eliminate the drug crossing the border.
item.dc:creator: JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Navy fighter jet crashes into San Diego Bay near naval air station; 2 pilots rescued
item.description: Two military pilots were rescued after their Boeing EA-18G Growler jet crashed into the water in San Diego Bay on Wednesday morning. It's unclear what caused the Navy aircraft to crash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Military plane crashes near San Diego: authorities
item.description: Emergency crews are responding to reports of a military plane that crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island, according to the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two alleged serial shoplifter banned from every Walmart in the USA
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- A serial shoplifter has gotten herself banned from every Walmart in the United States of America due to her history of stealing from the company, Memphis Police say. Ashley Cross, 37, is charged with criminal trespass and theft of merchandise less than $1,000.
item.dc:creator: Deja Davis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: As DOGE hammers away at the US government, Republicans stir with quiet objections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Katie Britt has been working to make sure the Trump administration's Department of Government Efficiency doesn't hit what she called “life-saving, groundbreaking research at high-achieving institutions,” including her state's beloved University of Alabama.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Coca-Cola ICE raids? Company faces backlash in face of unfounded rumors
item.description: (KRON) -- A significant backlash has emerged against soft drink manufacturer Coca-Cola in the wake of rumors that the company laid off its own immigrant employees and then called the U.S. Immigration and Customs and Enforcement Agency on them. Rumors have circulated on X, Reddit, and Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: School gym roof collapses under 350 tons of snow
item.description: A recent snowstorm that pummeled parts of Oregon caused the roof at a high school gym to collapse under the weight of about 700,000 pounds of snow, school officials said.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Christian aid groups weigh life-threatening choices about who to help after USAID funding pause
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a warehouse in Haiti, nearly four metric tons of seeds cannot be distributed. Soon the planting season will be gone and with it, the best chance for those seeds to produce emergency food.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Monty the giant schnauzer wins Westminster dog show
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — This time, the giant schnauzer went the full Monty. After coming close in the last two years, Monty the giant schnauzer won the top prize at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show Tuesday night, leaving handler and co-owner Katie Bernardin almost too emotional to speak.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Audio from Titan submersible implosion released by NOAA
item.description: In the 23-second recording, the sound of static can be heard, followed by a booming noise — then more static.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Georgia school shooting suspect's father granted bond as victims share emotional statements in court
item.description: A Georgia judge granted Apalachee High School shooting suspect Colt Gray's father, Colin Gray, a $500,000 bond. Colin Gray has pleaded not guilty to 29 criminal counts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 14 bands and artists nominated for Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: Mariah Carey, Chubby Checker, Cyndi Lauper, OutKast and Phish are some the 2025 nominees for induction into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, a list that also includes two sets of musical brothers who have had public feuds and recent reunions — the Black Crowes and Oasis.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republican lawmakers urge Trump to respect federal court rulings
item.description: GOP lawmakers are growing increasingly worried about the nation plunging into a constitutional crisis if Trump ignores court orders halting his most aggressive actions.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump doubles down on plan to empty Gaza. This is what he has said and what's at stake
item.description: Behind U.S. President Donald Trump’s vows to turn Gaza into a “Riviera of the Middle East” lies a plan to forcibly drive a population from its land, rights groups say, warning it could be a war crime under international law.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House fires USAID inspector general following critical report
item.description: Under the law, the administration is required to give 30-day notice to Congress prior to terminating an inspector general and give reasons why.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Migrant suspect in Texas cheerleader's murder confronted by victim's grieving mom in court
item.description: Rafael Govea Romero, the suspect in Texas cheerleader Lizbeth Medina's murder, appeared in court Feb 6, when a judge ruled he was competent to stand trial in August.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eastern storm cuts power to tens of thousands as California braces for flooding
item.description: Heavy snow and freezing rain from Kentucky to the nation's capital caused hundreds of crashes and left tens of thousands of residents in the dark.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley And John Raby, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope rebukes Trump administration over immigrant deportations and appears to aim directly at Vance
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis issued a major rebuke Tuesday to the Trump administration’s plans for mass deportations of migrants, warning that the forceful removal of people purely because of their illegal status deprives them of their inherent dignity and “will end badly.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Court sets March 19 date for first execution in Arizona in over 2 years
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The Arizona Supreme Court set a March 19 execution date Tuesday for a man who pleaded guilty to murder more than 17 years ago and recently said his death sentence was “long overdue.” It would be the state’s first application of the death penalty in more than two years.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man linked to Arizona teen Alicia Navarro sentenced to prison for child sexual abuse images
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — A Montana man who had been living with a teenager who disappeared from her home in Arizona years earlier was sentenced to decades in a prison for possession of child sex abuse material, authorities said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Detroit police say 2 kids died in a cold car after months without a permanent home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Two children who likely froze to death in a van outside a Detroit casino had been living in the vehicle with their family for a few months, moving around when possible, authorities said Tuesday. Their mother had contacted City Hall on Nov.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Roses are red, violets are blue, 940 million flowers are traveling (through Miami) to you
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers. In the run up to Feb. 14, agricultural specialists at Miami International Airport have processed about 940 million stems of cut flowers, according to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Super Bowl 'flu'? Record number of employees didn't go to work Monday, poll suggests
item.description: “May be calling out sick tomorrow,” one Eagles supporter wrote on X on Sunday night. “Got that birds flu.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge finds Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending
item.description: A federal judge found Monday that the Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending and told the White House to release all the money.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Schumer warns of 'Trump shutdown,' lays out 4-pronged plan for Democrats
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) in a letter to colleagues Monday warned of the possibility of a “Trump shutdown” and reminded fellow senators that Democrats have the power to make or break any bill to fund the government past March 14.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Rudd, Edwards named newest NASCAR Hall of Fame inductees
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — One of the toughest drivers in NASCAR history joined one the most enigmatic drivers of the modern era as the newest inductees into the Hall of Fame in a star-studded Friday night ceremony.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: HIV infections could jump over 6 times if US support dropped & not replaced, UNAIDS chief says
item.description: The head of the U.N. AIDS agency said Monday the number of new HIV infections could jump more than six times by 2029 if American support of the biggest AIDS program is dropped, warning that millions of people could die and more resistant strains of the disease could emerge.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG AP Medical Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are working to recover the wreckage of a plane crash in western Alaska that killed 10 people, while investigators are trying to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, WGN 9
